docs.title: 社区服务
docs.text: • 7.3 Individual Reflective Journal

docs.title: PPT 2
docs.text:
Purpose of written communication

  • to persuade
  • to inform
  • to promote goodwill
  • to request

A well-structured text communicates the writer's intentions to the reader.

In such a text, the structure acts like a framework, in that it enables the material to be presented in a coherent and logical manner.

A text without structure, will lead the reader being lost or misunderstood.

PDA

  • purpose
  • detail
  • action

4Cs of communication

  • clarity
  • conclusion
  • coherence
  • continuity

docs.title: PPT 3
docs.text:
Rhetoric: The art of persuasive speaking or writing.

Using language effectively to persuade.

Rhetorical Triangle

  • ethos

    the writer establishes credibility/trustworthiness

    Appeal to Ethics and Credibility

  • pathos

    the writer appeals to the emotions of the audience

    Appeal to Emotions

  • logos

    the writer appeals to the audience’s sense of logic

    Appeal to Logic and Reason

docs.title: PPT 4
docs.text:
Audience

  • primary
  • secondary
  • tertiary

Or

  • internal

    • Subordinates 下级
    • Supervisors 上级
    • Peers 同级

  • external

Audience Analysis

  • Demographic Analysis
  • Situational Analysis
  • Psychographic Analysis

docs.title: PPT 5
docs.text:
direct approaches to writing

  • good
  • neutral
  • some bad

indirect approaches to writing

  • bad
  • buffer

direct approaches

  • P introduction (State main idea)
  • D body/content (Explain main idea)
  • A conclusion (Call to action & End with polite close)

indirect approaches

  • B buffer (Neutral statements)
  • D body/content (Explain main idea (provide reasoning/justification))
  • P main idea (State main idea)
  • A concludion (Call to action & End with polite close)

buffer

  • Be neutral
  • Don't exceed three short sentences
  • Don't imply a negative statement

docs.title: PPT 6
docs.text:
Language of Business Writing

  • avoid old-fashioned language
  • personalize your letter
  • keep reader in mind
  • use words that are simple and easy to understand
  • use 'active voice'
  • use neutral tone

Elements of a business letter

  • letterhead (optional)

  • return address (writer)

    • a company

    name + position + company's name + company's address

    • an individual writer

    name + home address

  • date

    October 27, 2026

    27 October 2026

    2026 October, 27

  • inside address (recipient)

    same as return address

  • salutation

    Dear Sir

    Dear Madam

    Dear Sir or Madam

    Dear Mr. James

    end with , or :

  • subject

    only one line

    SUBJECT: New Office Policy

  • body or text

    para: 3-5

    some can be 5+

    we/I

    PDA/BDPA

  • complimentary close

    Dear Sir, Dear Madam, or Dear Sir or Madam \(\rightarrow\) Yours faithfully

    Dear Mr. James \(\rightarrow\) Yours sincerely

    leave 1-3 lines between the last para and this one

  • signature

  • name and title lines

  • enclosure (optional)

    • Enc.
    • Encl.
    • Enc:
    • Encl:

docs.title: PPT 7
docs.text:
memorandum - memo

  • A business memo is a slightly short document used to transmit information within an organization.
  • Memos are characterized by being brief, direct, and easy to navigate.
  • They are less formal than letters but should maintain a professional style.

memo

  • traditional
  • simplified

traditional memo

To: (Audience – Individual or group)

From: (writer’s info – Name, Position)

Date: (Complete date should be written)

Subject: (Simple and direct)

simplified memo

5 June 2026 (Complete date should be written)

All staff (Audience – Individual or group)

Compulsory Training (Simple and direct)

para

number 2-4

general 2-3

Introduction Paragraph

main purpose + briefly explain

Body Paragraph

Explanation

Concluding Paragraph

General conclusion + Be direct

Finally

Keep memos brief and to the point

docs.title: PPT 8
docs.text:
AIDA PLAN

  • Attention (opening)

    introduce your product

  • Interest (content)

    make your audience clear

    provide complete information

  • Desire (content)

    provide evidence/proof of your product’s efficiency

  • Action (closure)

    provide actions or steps

use postscript

P.S is useful as the last attention grabber

grab the audience attention by providing “extra” content

docs.title: PPT 9
docs.text:
Three-Step Writing Process

Step 1: Analyzing the Situation

Profile Your Audience

Step 1: Gathering Information

Step 1: Selecting right Medium

oral written visual eletronic

factors

  • Medium richness
  • Message formality
  • Medium limitations
  • Urgency
  • Cost
  • Audience preferences

Step 1: Organizing your Ideas

Step 2: Writing

adapt to your audience

compose your message

Step 3: Completing

Revise your message

Proofread the final product

Distribute your message

docs.title: PPT 10
docs.text:
BAD NEWS

direct

  • P Bad news
  • D Reasons
  • A Polite close

indirect

  • buffer
  • reasons
  • bad news
  • positive close

urgent bad news can be direct

docs.title: PPT 11
docs.text:
Thank-you - goodwill

Condolence - goodwill

Congratulations - good news

Recommendation - good news/neutral

Inquiry - neutral

Order and acknowledgment - neutral

direct approach

  • Open with the good or neutral news (main idea/purpose)
  • Follow with adequate details or descriptions
  • End pleasantly (Action)

docs.title: PPT 12
docs.text:
1. To: recipient's email
  2. Cc: carbon copy/courtesy copy
  3. Bcc: blind carbon copy/blind courtesy copy
  4. Subject
  5. Attachments
  6. Greetings
  7. Content: 2-5

  8. Sign-offs

    Yours faithfully, (for general greeting)

    Yours sincerely, (for specific greeting)

    Best Regards, (for specific greeting)

    Regards, (for specific greeting)

    Best wishes, and etc. (for specific greeting)

  9. Signature (optional)

  10. Name (mandatory)
  11. Title/Position (based on situation)
  12. Company name (additional)
  13. Phone/Fax (additional)

docs.title: PPT 13
docs.text:
Job application

FORMAT

  • Return address
  • Date
  • Inside address
  • Salutation
  • Subject
  • Body of letter
  • Complimentary close
  • Signature
  • Name
  • Enclosure:
  • Resume/CV

CONTENT

The opening paragraph (purpose)

The middle paragraphs (explanation)

The closing paragraph (action)

Content

must be clear, complete and correct

Language used and expressions/tone

must be formal, be convincing and sound confident

docs.title: PPT 14
docs.text:
CV

PERSONAL INFORMATION (compulsory)

EDUCATIONAL BACKGROUND & ACADEMIC QUALIFICATIONS (compulsory)

WORK EXPERIENCE (compulsory)

SKILLS (hard skill + soft skill) (compulsory)

REFERENCES (compulsory)

CAREER OBJECTIVE / STATEMENT OF PROFILE / PROFESSION STATEMENT (additional)

EXTRA-CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES (additional)

PERSONAL INTERESTS OR HOBBIES (additional)

SALARY INFORMATION (additional)

VOLUNTARY WORK, PERSONAL CHARACTERISTICS and etc.

PERSONAL INFORMATION

  • Full name
  • Address
  • Contact number
  • E-mail address

EDUCATIONAL BACKGROUND & ACADEMIC QUALIFICATIONS

from new to old

WORK EXPERIENCE

from new to old

docs.title: PPT 1
docs.text:
National anti-corruption plan 2019-2023

This 5-year plan NACP was launched on 2019.1.29

it serves as policy and directive

AMAR = Anti-corruption Student Force

STUDENT ANTI-CORRUPTION VOLUNTEER CORPS 2021.12.3

SPRM = MACC = Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission

2021.4.19 decide to add it to courses

elective subjects 2021

compulsory courses 2023

docs.title: PPT 2
docs.text:
No integrity is the main cause of curruption

Integrity is doing the right thing, even when no one is watching.

5 factors which affect integrity:

  1. individual weaknesses
  2. poor leadership
  3. weaknesses in systems and procedures
  4. unhealthy culture
  5. structure and institutions

10 reasons why individual commit corruption and abuse of power:

  1. lack integrity
  2. human nature that loves being celebrated
  3. human nature that wants easy money
  4. practice and tradition in organization
  5. conspiracy and mutual agreement
  6. good relations
  7. start with a small amount
  8. tempted with bribes multiple times
  9. managed to reject bribes, but not forever
  10. contractor's tactics

mindset of curruption offenders

  1. why do I take bribes?
  2. ambitious and greedy
  3. victim of circumstance and fear of being isolated
  4. leadership by example
  5. "get away once, get away forever" mindset
  6. frustation syndrome
  7. for survival

high risk fields

  1. contract and tender
  2. purchase and supply
  3. enforcement and regulation
  4. office property and asset

docs.title: *PPT 3
docs.text:
corruption = receiving or giving gratification(bribe) to do something related to his official duties

elements of corruption:

  1. giver
  2. receiver
  3. gratification(bribery act)
  4. response
  5. mens rea 犯罪意图

CPI = corruption perception index

MACC = Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission

it lead agency in the effort of curbing corruption in Malaysia

the timeline

  • 1959 anti-corruption unit under prime minister's department + criminal investigation department, royal Malaysia police + procecution division in ministry of justice
  • 1967 anti-corruption agency ACA
  • 1973 national bureau of investigation NBI
  • 1982 anti-corruption agency ACA
  • 2009 MACC

contains 5 departments

  • Special Committee on Corruption
  • Anti-Corruption Advisory Board
  • Complaints Committee
  • Consultation and Corruption Prevention Panel
  • Operations Review Panel

MACC's functions

  • receive complaints regarding corruption case and abuse of position
  • conduct investigation
  • provide consultancy and advisory sevice to improvise systems and procedures which can lead to oppotunities and avenue to corruption
  • educate and foster community support in curbing corruption

complaints \(\rightarrow\) MACC: investigation \(\rightarrow\) attorneys general's chambers: prosecution \(\rightarrow\) court: judgement

MACC's three-pronged strategy

  • emforcement
  • prevention
  • education

anti-corruption warriors WAR

begins at 2014

other agencies

  • GIACC 2018.6.1

    coordinate and monitor all activities related to governance, integrity and anti-corruption

  • EAIC

    Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission

docs.title: PPT 4
docs.text:
4 corruption offences

  1. solicit and accept bribe
  2. offer or give bribe
  3. false claim
  4. abuse of power by a public officer

corruption

imprisonment \(\leq\) 20 years

fine the value of gratification \(\times 5\) or RM 10000 whichever is higher

money laundering

imprisonment \(\leq\) 15 years

fine the value of gratification \(\times 5\) or RM 5M whichever is higher

receive gifts by a public servant

2 years' imprisonment or fine or both

gifts \(\rightarrow\) bribes

  • the giver and recipient of gifts has official relations
  • the giving of gifts influences the decision that will be made by the recipient
  • the value of the gift is not commensurate with the gift

docs.title: *PPT 5
docs.text:
white-collar crimes = crimes that do not involve violence, is financially motivated. Committed by professionals in the public and private sectors

blue-collar crimes = crimes committed by the lower social group, causing injuries and property damage

white-collar crimes

  • fraud 诈骗
  • accounting fraud 会计造假
  • misappropriation 挪用公款
  • ponzi scheme 庞氏骗局
  • cyber crimes 网络犯罪

current crimes

  • macau scam
    • lucky draw
    • police officer/ government agency 'spoofing'
    • bank negara or commercial bank officer 'spoofing'
    • kidnapping scam and demanding ransom
  • love scam
  • parcel scam
  • e-banking scam
    • fraudulent applications
    • account takeover/ indentity theft
    • non-received issue fraud
  • phishing
  • credit card scam

main cause of fraud = weakness in internal controls leads to fraud being committed in organizations

corruption is a type of white-collar crime

docs.title: *PPT 6
docs.text:
POWER \(\rightarrow\) corruption

greed in one's self gives opportunities to commit corruption

power + opportunities - integrity = corruption

fraud triangle theory

pressure + opportunity + rationalisation

symptoms of corruption

  • sudden change into a lavish lifestyle
  • failure to explain source of wealth
  • refusal to be promoted or transferred
  • suspicious telephone calls
  • act like a rich person
  • sudden resignation
  • close relationship with company/ departments' customers

affect

  • national economy & reputation
  • individual & family
  • property
  • organisation
  • security & environment

docs.title: PPT 7
docs.text:
responsiblity to lodge a report

fail to report offer/giving of bribes

  • fine \(\leq\) RM 100000
  • or imprisonment \(\leq\) 10 years
  • or both

fail to report solicitation/receiving of bribes

  • fine \(\leq\) RM 10000
  • or imprisonment \(\leq\) 2 years
  • or both

whistle-blower 内部检举者

witness 证人

8 tips to avoid corruption

  1. do not say yes on first offer
  2. be careful with compliments
  3. do not ever show actions or behaviour that seems to portray that we are asking
  4. do not get accustomed to receiving any form of entertainment from those who have official relationship with us, even if it is just a treat to a drink
  5. avoid a relationship that is too close with a company that has an official relationship with us
  6. decline offer or persuasion firmly
  7. avoid talking about personal matters or family issues with people with whom we have an official relations
  8. befriend people who are honest and can advice and guide us

docs.title: *PPT 8
docs.text:
2020.6.1 Section 17A MACC enforced

3 objectives

  1. to encourage business to be conducted with honesty and free from corruption
  2. to encourage commercial organisations to implement corruption prevention measures in the organisation
  3. to promote good governance and compliance with anti-corruption laws

corporate liability

a company or commercial organisation is punished for allowing its officer or employee to commit corruption to obtain a project

4 types of commercial organisation

  1. organisations incorporated under companies act
  2. businesses registered under partnership act
  3. businesses registered limited liability partnership act
  4. overseas companies/ businesses conducting business in Malaysia

if found

  • fine \(\geq\) 10 times of the value of gratification, or RM 1M, whichever is higher
  • or imprisonment \(\leq\) 20 years
  • or both

TRUST principle

  • Top level commitment
  • Risk assessment
  • Undertake control measures
  • Systematic review, monitoring & enforcement
  • Training & communication

docs.title: Week 1 Logic
docs.text:
proposition 命题

  • 已经确认T或F

truth value 真值

  • 求命题是T或F

truth table 真值表

negation 取反

  • \( eg p = not~p\)

conjunction 和

  • \(p\land q = p~and~q\)

disjunction 或

  • inclusive or 意为可以都T
  • \(p\lor q = p~or~q\)

XOR 异或

  • exclusive or 意为不能都是T
  • \(p\oplus q=p~xor~q\)

一般or表示\(\lor\)而不是\(\oplus\)

conditional statement 条件语句

  • \(p ightarrow q\)

    只有当p为T，q为F时，该式子为F

\(p ightarrow q\) 的说法

  • 正向 p...q

    p implies q

    p only if q

    p is sufficient for q

  • 逆向 q...p

    q whenever p

    q follows from p

    q if p

    q is necessary for p

\(p ightarrow q = eg p \lor q\)

converse 逆命题

  • pq交换

inverse 否命题

  • pq都取反

contra-positive 逆否命题

  • pq既交换也都取反

在求这些命题之前可以先把题目转换成if-then格式

equivalent 等价的

  • 两个命题如果总是有一样的真值，则称他们等价

一个命题与它的逆否命题等价

biconditional statement 双条件语句

  • \(p\leftrightarrow q\)

    只有当pq相同时，这个语句才为真

precedence 优先级

  • \( eg\)
  • \(\land \lor\)
  • \( ightarrow \leftrightarrow\)

tautology 重言式

  • 一个命题恒为T

contradiction 矛盾式

  • 一个命题恒为F

logic equivalent 逻辑等价

  • 当 \(p\leftrightarrow q\) 是tautology

commutative law 交换律

  • \(p\land q = q\land p\)

associative law 结合律

  • \(p\land (q\land r) = (p\land q)\land r = p\land q\land r\)

distributive law 分配率

  • \(p\land (q\lor r) = (p\land q)\lor (p\land r)\)

内外符号相同符合结合律，符号不同符合分配率

De Morgan's Law

\( eg (p\lor q)= eg p\land eg q\)

\( eg (p\land q)= eg p\lor eg q\)

想要判断是否等价：

  1. 真值表
  2. 逻辑等价语句

satisfiable

  • 如果有一个取值能使命题为真，则称它是satisfiable

unsatisfiable = contradiction

docs.title: Week 2 Predicates
docs.text:
predicate 谓词

  • proposition function 命题方程

universal quantification 全称命题

  • for all 可以满足
  • \( orall\)

counter-example 反例

existential quantification 存在命题

  • there exists one 可以满足
  • \(\exists\)

\(\exists!\) 表示只存在 如果定义域有无穷多个数只能用 \( orall \exists\) 而不能用 \(\land \lor\)

优先级

  • \( orall \exists\)
  • \( eg\)
  • \(\land \lor\)
  • \( ightarrow \leftrightarrow\)

\( orall x (P(x)\land Q(x)) = ( orall x P(x))\land ( orall x Q(x))\)

\(\exists x (P(x)\lor Q(x)) = (\exists x P(x))\lor (\exists x Q(x))\)

De Morgan's laws for quantifiers

\( eg ( orall x P(x)) = \exists x eg P(x)\)

\( eg (\exists x P(x)) = orall x eg P(x)\)

The order of quantifiers

\( orall x orall y P(x,y) = orall y orall x P(x,y)\)

\(\exists x \exists y P(x,y) = \exists y \exists x P(x,y)\)

docs.title: Week 3 Rule of inference
docs.text:
argument 论证

  • 很多premises前提得出一个conclusion结论
  • 若它是tautology，则论证valid
  • 如果不是，则论证是incorrect reasoning或fallacy

There are some tautology

the law of detachment

  1. modus ponens 肯定前键

    \(((p ightarrow q)\land p) ightarrow q\)

  2. modus tollens 否定后键

    \(((p ightarrow q)\land eg q) ightarrow eg p\)

hypothetical syllogism 假言三段式

\(((p ightarrow q)\land (q ightarrow r)) ightarrow (p ightarrow r)\)

disjunctive syllogism 析取三段式

\(((p\lor q)\land eg p) ightarrow q\)

addition

\(p ightarrow (p\lor q)\)

simplification

\((p\land q) ightarrow p\)

conjunction

\(((p)\land (q)) ightarrow (p\land q)\)

universal instantiation

  • 全称命题可以推出存在命题

universal generalization

  • 全部满足可以推出全称命题

universal modus ponens

\( orall c ( orall x (P(x) ightarrow Q(x))\land P(c)) ightarrow Q(c)\)

existential instantiation

  • 存在命题可以推出有部分可以满足

existential generalization

  • 有一些满足可以推出存在命题

docs.title: Week 4 Sets
docs.text:
set 集合

  • unordered + distinct
  • 无序 + 不重复
  • \(a\in A\)

roster method 列举法

set builder notations 描述法

cardinality \(|S|\)

表示为集合有多少个元素，也就是size

empty set \(\varnothing\)

singleton set \(|S|=1\)

boolean set \(=\{0,1\}\)

interval 区间

proper subset 真子集

  • 不包含equal相等情况

power set

  • 所有子集构成的集合

\(|P(S)| = 2^{|S|}\)

cartesian product

\(A\times B = \{(a,b)|a\in A, b\in B\}\)

\((a,b)\) is ordered

\(|A\times B| = |A|\cdot |B|\)

union 并集

intersection 交集

  • 如果他们的交集是空集，则他们disjoint

\(|A\cup B| = |A|+|B|-|A\cap B|\)

difference 差集

\(A\setminus B\)

complement 补集

\(\overline{A} = U\setminus A\)

De Morgan's Laws

\(\overline{A\cap B} = \overline{A}\cup \overline{B}\)

\(\overline{A\cup B} = \overline{A}\cap \overline{B}\)

docs.title: Week 5 Functions
docs.text:
function 方程

  • mappings or transformations
  • 从每一个a映射到恰好一个b
  • \(\forall a\in A, \exists! b\in B,f(a)=b\)

\(A\) 是domain/source set

\(B\) 是codomain/target set

\(b\) 是image of \(a\)

\(a\) 是preimage of \(b\)

\((g\circ f)(a) = g(f(a))\)

以上的前提是f的值域等于g的定义域

\(G_f=\{(a,b)|a\in A, b=f(a)\}\subseteq A\times B\)

\(\lfloor x\rfloor\) 下取整

\(\lceil x\rceil\) 上取整

\(S\subseteq A\)

\(f(S)=\{t\in B|\exists s\in S, t=f(s)\}\)

\(f(S)=\{f(s)|s\in S\}\)

\(T\subseteq B\)

\(f^{-1}(T)=\{s\in A|f(s)\in T\}\)

\(f^{-1}(T)\) 只是一个表示不能说明 \(f\) 有 \(f^{-1}\)

成为函数的两个要求

  1. \(\forall a\in A, \exists b\in B, f(a)=b\)
  2. \(\forall a_1,a_2\in A, a_1=a_2\rightarrow f(a_1)=f(a_2)\)

第一个解决from each，第二个解决unique

injective 单射

  • \(\forall a_1,a_2\in A, f(a_1)=f(a_2)\rightarrow a_1=a_2\)
  • 理解为每一个因变量都只由一个自变量映射得到
  • 与平行与x轴的线的交点数 \(\leq 1\)

严格单调必定单射

surjective 满射

  • \(\forall b\in B, \exists a\in A, f(a)=b\)
  • 理解为所有在值域中的数都被映射到
  • 与平行于x轴的线的交点数 \(\geq 1\)

bijective = injective + surjective 双射

\(|A| = |B|\leftrightarrow\) 双射

injective \(|A|\leq |B|\)

surjective \(|A|\geq |B|\)

countable

  • cardinality是有限的或者和 \(\mathbb{N}\) 一样

infinitely countable

  • cardinality和 \(\mathbb{N}\) 一样

uncountable 不可数的

有关countable一些性质

  • \(|A|\leq |B|\) 并且 \(B\) 是可数的，则 \(A\) 也可数
  • \(|A|\leq |B|\) 并且 \(A\) 是不可数的，则 \(B\) 也不可数
  • \(A\) 和 \(B\) 都是可数的，\(A\cap B\) 和 \(A\cup B\) 也是可数
  • \(A\) 不可数，\(B\) 可数，则 \(A\setminus B\) 不可数

  • \(A\) 是可数的，\(f\) 是surjective，则 \(B\) 也是可数的

    也就是\(A\) 是可数的，则 \(f(A)\) 也是可数的

  • \(A\) 和 \(B\) 都是可数的，则 \(A\times B\) 是可数的

\(\mathbb{NZQ}\) 都可数

\(\mathbb{R}\) 不可数

docs.title: Week 6 Number theory
docs.text:
\(a\) devides \(b\) 就是a整除b

\(a\) 是factor/divisor of \(b\)

\(b\) 是multiple of \(a\)

\(a\mid b\)

\(b\) is divisible by \(a\) 就是b可以被a整除

\(a\mid b\) 和 \(a\mid c\) 可以推出 \(a\mid (b+c)\)

\(a\mid b\) 可以推出 \(a\mid bc\) 其中 \(c\) 是整数

\(d\) 是正整数

在 \(0\leq r< d\) 时，有 \(a = dq+r\)

\(q\) 是商quotient

\(r\) 是余数remainder

congruent 同余

如果 \(m\mid (a-b)\)

\(a\equiv b\pmod{m}\)

\(a\) is congruent to \(b\) modulo \(m\)

congruence relation 同余关系

\(m\) 是modulus

\(\{mk+a|k\in \mathbb{Z}\}\) 是congruence class of \(a\) modulo \(m\)

\(a\equiv b\pmod{m}\) 和 \(c\equiv d\pmod{m}\)

可以推出

  • \(a+c\equiv b+d\pmod{m}\)
  • \(ac\equiv bd\pmod{m}\)
  • \(a^r\equiv b^r\pmod{m}\)

base \(b\) expansion

\(m=(d_kd_{k-1}...d_1d_0)_b\)

\(m=\sum_{j=0}^k d_j b^j\) 其中 \(0\leq d_j< b\)

fast modular exponentiation 快速幂

想要求\(a^k\)

  1. 对 \(k\) 进行二进制分解
  2. 算 \(a, a^2, a^4, ...\)
  3. 最后乘在一起

prime number 质数

composite number 合数

1既不是质数也不是合数

The Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic

\(n=p_1^{k_1}p_2^{k_2}...p_m^{k_m}\)

\(p_i\) 是prime factors

\(k_i\) 是正整数

gcd 最大公因数

如果gcd为1，则他们互质relatively prime/coprime

The Euclidean Algorithm 求最大公因数

\(a=r_0, b=r_1\)

\(a=bq_1+r_2\)

\(b=r_2q_2+r_3\)

\(r_2=r_3q_3+r_4\)

\(...\)

\(r_{n-2}=r_{n-1}q_{n-1}+r_n\)

\(r_{n-1}=r_nq_n\)

最大公因数就是 \(r_n\)

Bezout's identity 裴蜀定理

\(a\) 和 \(b\) 是正数

\(gcd(a,b)=sa+tb\) 存在有一组解\(s,t\)

Extended Euclidean Algorithm 拓展欧几里得

求解 \(gcd(a,b)=sa+tb\) 就是直接先算出gcd

然后再按照欧几里得的算法反向递推回去

\(r_n=r_{n-2}-r_{n-1}q_{n-1}\)

然后将 \(r_i\) 全部变为 \(a\) 和 \(b\)

如果 \(c\) 和 \(m\) 互质，则 \(ac\equiv bc\pmod{m}\rightarrow a\equiv b\pmod{m}\)

\(ac\equiv bc\pmod{m}\rightarrow a\equiv b\pmod{\frac{m}{gcd(c,m)}}\)

\(ab\equiv 1\pmod{m}\)

那么说 \(a\) is invertible modulo \(m\)

a在模m下可逆元

\(b\) is an inverse of \(a\) modulo \(m\)

b是a模m下的逆元

\(b\equiv a^{-1}\pmod{m}\)

\(a\) 模 \(m\) 可逆 \(\leftrightarrow\) \(gcd(a,m)=1\)

求 \(a\) 对 \(b\) 的逆元

  1. 求解 \(1=sa+tb\)
  2. 两边同时取模 \(b\)
  3. \(s\) 就是答案

pairwise relatively prime/pairwisely coprime 两两互质

Chinese Remainder Theorem 中国剩余定理

\(m_i\) 两两互质

由若干个 \(x\equiv a_i\pmod{m_i}\) 组成的方程组

\(x=(\sum a_i(M/m_i)y_i)~mod~M\)

\(y_i\) 是 \(M/m_i\) 对 \(m_i\) 的逆元

企鹅
docs.text: https://www.luogu.com.cn/problem/P14452

docs.title: 题目描述
docs.text:
有 \(n\) 只企鹅站在数轴上。第 \(i\) 只企鹅最初位于坐标 \(a_i\) 处。保证所有的 \(a_i\) 互不相同。

每只企鹅都会选择一个目标企鹅，记为 \(t_i\)（\(1 \leq t_i \leq n\)，且 \(t_i \neq i\)）。在时间 \(0\) 时，所有企鹅同时开始移动。每只企鹅以每秒 \(0.5\) 个单位长度的速度，朝着其目标企鹅当前所在的位置移动。

当第 \(i\) 只企鹅与它的目标企鹅 \(t_i\) 相遇时（即二者在同一时刻到达同一坐标），它会立即停止移动。对于每只企鹅，请你求出它停止移动的时间。

可以证明，每只企鹅都会在有限的时间内停止，且停止时间总是一个整数。

docs.title: 输入格式
docs.text:
输入的第一行包含一个整数 \(n\)（\(2 \leq n \leq 5 \cdot 10^5\)），表示企鹅的数量。

第二行包含 \(n\) 个整数 \(t_1, t_2, \ldots, t_n\)（\(1 \leq t_i \leq n\)，且 \(t_i \neq i\)），其中 \(t_i\) 表示第 \(i\) 只企鹅选择的目标企鹅编号。

第三行包含 \(n\) 个互不相同的整数 \(a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_n\)（\(-5 \cdot 10^8 \leq a_i \leq 5 \cdot 10^8\)），其中 \(a_i\) 表示第 \(i\) 只企鹅的初始坐标。

docs.title: 输出格式
docs.text: 输出一行，包含 \(n\) 个整数，其中第 \(i\) 个整数表示第 \(i\) 只企鹅停止移动的时间（单位为秒）。

docs.title: 输入 #1
docs.text:
Text Only
3
2 3 1
-1 2 3

docs.title: 输出 #1
docs.text:
Text Only
7 1 4

docs.title: 输出 #2
docs.text:
Text Only
25 21 17 14 14 49 29 9 61 17

docs.title: 说明/提示
docs.text:
在第一个样例中：

最初，第 2 只企鹅位于第 1 只企鹅的右侧，因此第 1 只企鹅向右移动。同样地，第 2 只企鹅也向右移动，而第 3 只企鹅向左移动。

在第 \(1\) 秒时，第 2 只企鹅与第 3 只企鹅在 \(x = 2.5\) 处相遇，此时第 2 只企鹅停止移动。

此时，第 1 只企鹅的位置是 \(x = -0.5\)，第 2 只企鹅的位置是 \(x = 2.5\)，二者之间的距离为 \(3\)。由于第 1 只企鹅以每秒 \(0.5\) 个单位的速度移动，它还需要 \(6\) 秒才能到达第 2 只企鹅。因此，第 1 只企鹅在第 \(7\) 秒时停止。

在第 1 只企鹅停止之前，第 3 只企鹅会在第 \(4\) 秒与它相遇。因此，答案分别为 \(7\)、\(1\) 和 \(4\)。

docs.title: 建立模型
docs.text:
\(n\)个点\(n\)条边，每个点只有一条出边，表示起点的目标是终点。

我们不难发现当一个点的答案只由自己目标节点决定

所以可以考虑\(DP\)

但是\(n\)个点\(n\)条边

意味着每个连通块内都有且仅有一个环

所以考虑断掉环上的一条边\(i\)使得该连通块变为一棵树

这棵树是以\(i\)的终点\(root\)为根节点

现在这个问题拆分成了两个子问题：

  1. \(root\)的答案怎么算？
  2. 树上\(DP\)如何递推？

首先先来解决问题一

为了保证root答案的正确性

需要在环上选择最早停止的企鹅

因为最早停止的企鹅停止时双方一定满足

  • 相向而行
    • 反证法
    • 因为如果最早停止的是同向而行的企鹅
    • 在前面的企鹅必然要先停下才能被后面的企鹅追上
    • 前面的企鹅先停下与假设矛盾
  • 双方在上一个时刻都在行动
    • 反证法
    • 因为如果有一方已经停止
    • 与假设最早停止矛盾

在环上所有\(u\rightarrow v\)，且\(u\)和\(v\)相向而行中寻找两个距离最接近的即\(abs(a_u-a_v)\)最小

假设距离最小的一对是\(u_0\rightarrow v_0\)

\(root=u_0\)

\(ans_{root}=abs(a_{u_0}-a_{v_0})\)

至此算出了\(root\)的答案

接着来解决问题二

针对任一对\(u\rightarrow v\)

假设当前得知\(v\)的答案

考虑是否可以推出\(u\)的答案

分两种情况

  1. \(u\)和\(v\)同向而行
  2. \(u\)和\(v\)相向而行
\[ dist=u和v之间的距离=abs(a_u-a_v)\\ Situation~1:ans_u=2\times dist+ans_v\\ Situation~2:ans_u=max(dist,2\times dist-ans_v) \]

docs.title: 代码实现
取石子
docs.text: https://codeforces.com/contest/2181/problem/F

docs.title: 题意
docs.text:
有\(n\)堆石头，第\(i\)堆有\(a_i\)个石头。

Alice和Bob轮流执行以下操作

  • 对手帮自己指定一堆非空石头
  • 自己从被指定的石头堆中取任意个石头

已知Alice先手，且双方绝顶聪明，取光所有石头的一方获胜

\(T\)组数据，判断谁会获胜

输入

Text Only
3
3
1 2 3
1
1
5
10 3 4 7 4

输出

Text Only
Bob
Alice
Alice

样例一

  • Bob选择第一堆，Alice从中拿了一个
  • Alice选择第三堆，Bob从中拿了两个
  • Bob选择第三堆，Alice从中拿了一个
  • Alice选择第二堆，Bob从中拿了两个，获胜

docs.title: 思路
docs.text:
对方给自己指定了一堆个数大于1的石子，则自己必赢

为什么？

分类讨论：两种情况

  1. 如果当前状况除去这堆石子的状态是先手必赢

    那么自己就可以取到只剩一个石子

    然后指定对手去取这堆

    那么对手就必须只能取这个

    那么先手必赢的局面就是自己得到

  2. 如果当前状况除去这堆石子的状态是先手必输

    那么自己就可以把这堆所有都取完

    然后就把先手必输的局面交给对手

    所以自己就是必赢局面

所以双方思路都是就是

尽可能选择一堆个数等于1的给对方

就是只要看1的个数

注意：还要特殊考虑一下全都是1的情况

docs.title: 代码
老虎机
docs.text: https://codeforces.com/contest/2181/problem/J

docs.title: 题意
docs.text:
\(A\)有\(1000\)美元，去赌场玩老虎机，老虎机的中奖概率是由一个序列决定的

在制作这个老虎机时，随机从概率序列中选择一个\(a_i\)，并让这个老虎机中奖的概率为\(a_i\%\)

注意：老虎机只会有一个固定的中奖概率，且\(A\)不知道，\(A\)只知道这个序列

一共有\(k\)次游玩机会，每次游玩可以选择投入\(X\)，且不能贷款，\(X\)可以等于\(0\)

这个老虎机只会有两种结果

  • 中奖，收回\(2X\)
  • 未中奖，收回\(0\)

给定\(n,k\)以及

一个长度为\(n\)的序列，表示\(n\)个概率，分别为\(a_1,a_2,a_3,...,a_n\)，每个数表示的是中奖概率为\(a_i\%\)

\(k\)表示\(A\)最多可以玩的局数

docs.title: 思路
docs.text:
考虑一段固定长度为\(a+b\)的前缀序列，包含\(a\)次中奖、\(b\)次未中奖，对应概率为\(p^a(1-p)^b\)

因此可以等效看作存在一个自动机，按如下概率生成下一个结果为头奖：

\[p(a,b) = \frac{\sum p^{a+1}(1-p)^b}{\sum p^a(1-p)^b}\]

其中\(a\)为当前已中奖次数，\(b\)为当前未中奖次数

接下来定义状态\(dp(a,b,x)\)：表示当前已中奖\(a\)次、未中奖\(b\)次、持有资金\(x\)时，能获得的最大期望收益

状态转移方程为：

当\(a+b < k\)时：

\[dp(a,b,x)=\max_{\substack{0\leq y\leq x}}\big(p(a,b)\cdot dp(a+1,b,x+y)+(1-p(a,b))\cdot dp(a,b+1,x-y)\big)\]

当\(a+b = k\)时：

\[dp(a,b,x) = x\]

易得最优策略仅为全额下注或不下注

于是可设\(dp(a,b,x)=C[a][b]\cdot x\)，递推公式改写为：

当\(a+b < k\)时：

\[C[a][b] = \max\big(p(a,b)C[a+1][b]+(1-p(a,b))C[a][b+1],\ 2p(a,b)C[a+1][b]\big)\]

当\(a+b = k\)时：

\[C[a][b]=1\]

docs.title: 代码
限号出行
docs.text: https://codeforces.com/gym/106565/problem/I

docs.title: 题意
docs.text:
给定 \(m\) 条规则，形如 \((x_i,y_i)\) ，表示 \(x_i\) 字符后面不能紧跟着 \(y_i\)

有一个长度为 \(n\) 的字符串，要求从中删除 \(n-k\) 个字符，使得剩下的 \(k\) 个字符组成的字符串满足这 \(m\) 条规则，如果有多解，输出字典序最小，如果无解输出 \(-1\)

docs.title: 思路
docs.text:
考虑逆向DP

\(dp[i][j]\) 表示第 \(j\) 位到末尾这一段区间内，以 \(i\) 开头的合法串的最长长度

初始化就是，对于 \(j\) 位 \(dp_{s_j,j}=1\) ，其他均为 \(0\)

然后开始逆向递推

逆向对于每一个 \(i\) ，去枚举每一个 \(j\) 字母，可以进行以下递推，意为不选择第 \(i\) 位的字母，第 \(i\) 位直接从 \(i+1\) 继承过来

\(dp_{j,i}=max(dp_{j,i},dp_{j,i+1})\)

其次如果想要选择第 \(i\) 位的字母，要满足第 \(i\) 位的字母与第 \(i+1\) 位的 \(j\) 字母没有任何规则限制，则有以下递推

\(dp_{s_i,i}=max(dp_{s_i,i},dp_{j,i+1}+1)\)

计算答案就设置一个指针 \(i\) 最初指向 \(0\) ，从最终字符串第一位开始枚举 \(now\) 是否可以是 \(a,b,c,...\) 找到第一个满足 \(dp_{i,now}\geq k\) 的字母，然后把指针从 \(0\) 往后扫找到第一个 \(s_i = now\) 的 \(i\) ，然后把指针挪到 \(i+1\) 的位置，然后把 \(k\) 减一，循环往复直到找齐 \(k\) 个字母，能让字典序最小

docs.title: 代码
青青草原扛把子
docs.text: https://codeforces.com/gym/106565

docs.title: 题意
docs.text: 给定一个正方形的边长 \(side\) ，已知该正方形的边上若干个点的具体坐标 \((x_i,y_i)\) ，要求从中选取 \(k\) 个点，使得任意两点之间的最小曼哈顿距离尽可能大，要求这个最小曼哈顿距离的最大值。

docs.title: 题意转化
docs.text:
先考虑 \(d\) 作为最小曼哈顿距离的最大值，可以得到 \(d\leq side\)

反证法证明易得：由于至少有四个点，在边上有四个点，意味着在边上相邻的两个点之间的最短边上距离 \(\leq side\)

最短曼哈顿距离 \(\leq\) 最短边上距离

最短曼哈顿距离 \(\leq side\)

考虑对边正对着两点之间距离正好为 \(side\)

对于这种情况运用反证法

假设没有这一对点的替代方案，即两个点的左右边上距离 \(\leq side\) 都没有其他点

假设两点之间的边上距离分别为 \(d_1,d_2\) ，那么有 \(d_1+d_2 = side\times 4\)

不难看出如果 \(d_i\leq side\times 2\) ，则在 \(d_i\) 上则不会有其他点

因为 \(d_1+d_2 = side\times 4\) ，所以必有一个 \(d_i\leq side\times 2\)

即必有一边不会有其他点，又因为至少有四个点，所以另外两个点只能在一侧 \(d_i-side\times 2\) 的空隙中

又因为 \(d_i\leq side\times 3\) ，所以 \(d_i-side\times 2\leq side\)

在一个 \(\leq side\) 的空隙中，塞下两个点

这两个点之间的距离肯定 \(\leq side\)

则找到了原先那一对点的替代品，使得得到答案的那对点不会出现在对边

综上所述，结论：产生答案的那一对点一定是在同边或者邻边，且距离 \(\leq side\)

也就是说曼哈顿距离 \(=\) 边上距离

所以可以把问题拆成一条线，从 \((0,0)\) 开始逆时针一直到 \((0,1)\) ，总长度为 \(side\times 4\)

可以把这条线复制一遍，因为其实是一个环

问题就是在这个环上找 \(k\) 个点使得任意两点中最小的距离最大

docs.title: 思路
docs.text:
采用二分答案

计算在二分出来的答案 \(mid\) 下，是否可以找 \(k\) 个点使得任意两点中最小的距离超过 \(mid\)

也就是可以处理出来每个点后面最近的 \(\geq mid\) 的点

然后答案就是看起点往后跳 \(k-1\) 次之后得到的点与起点的第二个复制点的距离是否 \(\leq mid\)

往后跳 \(k-1\) 次的效果可以用ST表预处理的结果快速得到

如果上面问题的答案是肯定的，那么该距离 \(mid\) 成立，可以去找有没有更大的 \(mid\)

反之去找更小的
